fundamentals.name: Spotify Technology S.A.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Spotify Technology S.A., together with its subsidiaries, provides audio streaming subscription services worldwide. It operates in two segments, Premium and Ad-Supported. The Premium segment offers online and offline streaming access to its catalog of music and podcasts, including video, lossless music, and audiobooks in select markets through subscription offerings primarily sold directly to end users and partners. The Ad-Supported segment provides limited on-demand online access to its catalog of music and online and offline access to its catalog of podcasts on computers, tablets, mobile devices, and other smart devices. The company also offers sales, distribution and marketing, contract research and development, and customer and other support services. Spotify Technology S.A. was incorporated in 2006 and is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden.

judgment.summary: Strongly growing company with expanding margins and a healthy balance sheet, trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 18.0%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Earnings growth 222.4% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 220.4% indicate significant profitability expansion.
Operating margin at 15.8% and profit margin at 15.4% show strong operational efficiency.
Return on equity at 38.0% and return on assets at 11.6% demonstrate effective use of capital.
Total cash of $6.29B and a current ratio of 2.06 suggest good liquidity.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (769.9%) and fiscal year (22.4%) are very high.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 5.94 is high, though offset by substantial cash reserves.
Forward P/E of 27.22 and PEG ratio of 1.75 indicate a premium valuation.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 5.89 is also on the higher side.

news.paragraph: Spotify Technology (SPOT) has seen a significant surge in its stock price, driven by the recent launch of paid AI Remixes and Superfan tools, as well as a collaboration with Universal Music Group on AI remixes. The company's stock is up over 15% since its last earnings report, which also saw a substantial beat on EPS. This positive momentum is attracting attention from notable investors, with analysts maintaining a strong buy consensus and a high average target price.
